title: Headless Service for Direct Pod Access
description:
Create a headless Service (clusterIP: None) backed by 3 replicas. Use a busybox Pod to run nslookup and see DNS return multiple A records — one per Pod — instead of a single VIP.

**Why this matters**: Headless Services underpin StatefulSets and client-side load balancing. The DNS difference between ClusterIP and headless is key to running databases or message brokers on Kubernetes.

**Common Pitfalls**: nslookup may return fewer IPs if Pods are still starting. A headless Service with no matching selector returns zero DNS records.
steps: Set clusterIP: None in the Service to make it headless
Create a Deployment with 3 replicas labeled app=store so the headless Service selects them
Deploy a busybox Pod for DNS debugging
Wait for all 3 replicas: kubectl rollout status deployment/store
Run nslookup inside busybox: kubectl exec busybox-dns -- nslookup store-headless
Observe multiple A records returned — one per Pod IP
hints: Set spec.clusterIP: None to create a headless Service — this prevents VIP allocation
The headless Service selector must match the Deployment Pod template labels
Use image busybox:1.36 for the DNS debug pod with command ['sleep', '3600']
nslookup should return 3 Address lines when all 3 replicas are ready
If nslookup returns only 1-2 addresses, some Pods may still be in ContainerCreating

editor_config.initial_code:
# --- Headless Service (no ClusterIP) ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: store-headless
spec:
  clusterIP: # TODO: what value makes a Service headless?
  selector:
    app: store
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
---
# --- Deployment with 3 replicas ---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: store
spec:
  replicas: # TODO: how many replicas to demonstrate multiple DNS A records?
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: store
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: store
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: store
        image: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
# --- DNS debug Pod ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: busybox-dns
spec:
  containers:
  - name: busybox
    image: busybox:1.36
    command: # TODO: keep the pod alive so you can exec into it

_solution.code_solution:
# --- Headless Service (no ClusterIP) ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: store-headless
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: store
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
---
# --- Deployment with 3 replicas ---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: store
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: store
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: store
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: store
        image: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
# --- DNS debug Pod ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: busybox-dns
spec:
  containers:
  - name: busybox
    image: busybox:1.36
    command: ["sleep", "3600"]
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

echo "Waiting for store Deployment (3 replicas) to be ready..."
kubectl rollout status deployment/store --timeout=120s
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "✗ store Deployment did not become ready"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ store Deployment is ready"

CLUSTER_IP=$(kubectl get svc store-headless -o jsonpath='{.spec.clusterIP}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ "$CLUSTER_IP" != "None" ]; then
  echo "✗ Service store-headless has clusterIP='$CLUSTER_IP', expected 'None' (headless)"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Service store-headless is headless (clusterIP: None)"

EXPECTED_REPLICAS=3
READY_REPLICAS=$(kubectl get deployment store -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
if [ "$READY_REPLICAS" != "$EXPECTED_REPLICAS" ]; then
  echo "✗ Expected $EXPECTED_REPLICAS ready replicas, got $READY_REPLICAS"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Deployment has $READY_REPLICAS ready replicas"

ENDPOINT_COUNT=$(kubectl get endpoints store-headless -o jsonpath='{.subsets[0].addresses}' 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; data=sys.stdin.read(); addrs=json.loads(data) if data.strip() and data.strip()!='null' else []; print(len(addrs))" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
if [ "$ENDPOINT_COUNT" -lt 3 ]; then
  echo "✗ Expected at least 3 endpoints, got $ENDPOINT_COUNT"
  kubectl get endpoints store-headless
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Headless Service has $ENDPOINT_COUNT endpoints (one per Pod)"

echo "Waiting for busybox-dns Pod..."
timeout=60
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  phase=$(kubectl get pod busybox-dns -o jsonpath='{.status.phase}' 2>/dev/null || echo "Pending")
  if [ "$phase" = "Running" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 3
  ((elapsed+=3))
done
if [ "$phase" != "Running" ]; then
  echo "✗ busybox-dns Pod did not reach Running state"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ busybox-dns Pod is running"

DNS_OUTPUT=$(kubectl exec busybox-dns -- nslookup store-headless 2>/dev/null || echo "")
ADDRESS_COUNT=$(echo "$DNS_OUTPUT" | grep -c "^Address:" || true)

if [ "$ADDRESS_COUNT" -ge 3 ]; then
  echo "✓ DNS returned $ADDRESS_COUNT A records for store-headless (one per Pod)"
  echo "✓ Headless Service validation passed"
  exit 0
elif [ "$ADDRESS_COUNT" -ge 1 ]; then
  echo "✓ DNS returned $ADDRESS_COUNT A record(s) — some Pods may still be initializing"
  echo "✓ Headless Service DNS is working (returning Pod IPs, not a VIP)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "✗ nslookup returned no addresses for store-headless"
  echo "DNS output:"
  echo "$DNS_OUTPUT"
  exit 1
fi
